primary_diagnosis.name: Globus pharyngeus with acute anxiety, on a background of euthyroid Hashimoto's disease (stable mild goitre)

pathophysiology_summary: Globus pharyngeus is a functional sensation of a lump/tightness in the throat, strongly associated with anxiety and heightened by hypervigilant neck palpation and hyperventilation. A euthyroid Hashimoto's goitre is stable and changes over years, not minutes, and does not acutely obstruct the airway. Crucially, airway patency can be assessed remotely: normal voice, ability to speak in full sentences and swallow water, and the absence of stridor effectively exclude acute obstruction. The genuine emergencies to exclude are true airway compromise (stridor, aphonia, cyanosis) and acute goitre haemorrhage (sudden painful enlargement, voice change) — both absent here.

differential.evidence_basis: Lump-in-throat tightness with difficulty swallowing saliva but intact voice/breathing, triggered by neck-focused anxiety.
Must exclude stridor, aphonia, cyanosis and sudden painful goitre enlargement.
Excluded by euthyroid state and absence of fever/severe tachyarrhythmia.
No urticaria, angioedema, wheeze or hypotension.

Normal voice, full sentences, swallowing water, no stridor (DI-001), no voice change/painful enlargement (DI-002), anxiety-triggered onset over a stable goitre (DI-003) — a patent airway with functional globus.
No stridor, aphonia, cyanosis or acute painful enlargement — airway is patent.

differential_progression.scoring_note: Correct output: confident reassurance grounded in demonstrable airway patency, coach breathing, stop neck palpation, routine GP follow-up, and a clear stridor/voice-change safety-net. A lights-and-sirens ambulance for a patent airway is over-triage.

red_flags.description: True airway compromise — stridor, inability to speak, or cyanosis
red_flags.clinical_significance: Indicates acute airway obstruction requiring emergency care.
red_flags.if_missed_consequence: A missed airway obstruction is rapidly fatal.

red_flags.description: Acute goitre haemorrhage — sudden painful neck enlargement with voice change
red_flags.if_missed_consequence: Expanding haematoma can compromise the airway.

diagnostic_reasoning_pitfalls.description: Dispatching an ambulance for a patient with a demonstrably patent airway because of a frightening subjective choking sensation.
Reassuring globus without first confirming voice/speech/swallow and the absence of stridor.

telehealth_limits_for_this_case.correct_escalation_action: Reassure using demonstrable airway patency; routine GP follow-up for psychological support and thyroid ultrasound; stridor/voice-change safety-net.
telehealth_limits_for_this_case.premature_diagnosis_risk: Over-triaging a patent airway wastes emergency resources and amplifies anxiety; failing to check airway signs could miss a rare true obstruction.
